plainEnglish: This is the stricter Level AAA version of contrast: normal text needs at least 7:1 and large text at least 4.5:1 against its background. It's aimed at giving people with moderately low vision (around 20/80) readable text without assistive technology. The same exemptions as 1.4.3 apply (logos, disabled controls).
whyItMatters: Users with moderately low vision (around 20/80) need a 7:1 contrast ratio to read normal-size text comfortably without assistive technology.
quickCheck: Use Chrome DevTools' color picker and confirm the contrast meets the AAA threshold (7:1 for normal text, 4.5:1 for large); axe's color-contrast-enhanced rule checks AAA.
commonMistakes: Designing only to the AA 4.5:1 minimum when AAA conformance requires 7:1.
Mid-gray body text that passes AA but falls short of 7:1.
Brand colors used for text that can't reach 7:1 without adjustment.

codeExamples.label: Body text meeting the AAA 7:1 threshold
Body text that passes AA but fails AAA
